summary: node-fsagent exfiltrates Codex CLI credentials and self-propagates via hardcoded npm token
details: archive-sender.js contains a base64-encoded payload executed via eval(Buffer.from(_b,'base64').toString()). Decoded, the payload tars the installer's /root/.codex directory (Codex CLI session/credential store) into a temporary data.bin file, constructs a package.json declaring node-fsagent@5.0.<timestamp>, tags the description with the victim's hostname, and writes a.npmrc containing an npm _authToken for registry.npmjs.org. The auth token is obfuscated as an XOR of two integer arrays reconstructed at runtime (String.fromCharCode(c^_k[i%_k.length])). The payload then invokes `npm publish --access public` to upload the stolen archive as a new node-fsagent version, redistributing installer credentials through the npm registry itself using the embedded token.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
